methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $14.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.5B - $39.0B.

data.narrative:
Skild AI is a Series C–stage AI & Software company founded by Deepak Pathak and Abhinav Gupta, currently valued at $14.0B post-money on $1.4B+ total capital raised. The peer comparison set comprises four same-sector, same-stage companies (Figure AI, Luma AI, Runway, Magic AI) plus one public comparable (Palantir), providing a framework for stage-appropriate valuation benchmarking.

Two primary valuation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the $14.0B last post-money valuation, applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, implying $23.8B. Peer-median analysis across the four same-stage AI & Software companies yields a median valuation of $2.8B, though the peer set exhibits significant dispersion (range: $1.5B to $39.0B), reflecting volatility in frontier-tech valuations at this stage.

The triangulated analysis produces a median estimate of $13.3B with a low-to-high range of $2.3B to $27.4B. The current $14.0B valuation sits near the midpoint of this range, consistent with stage-progression methodology but notably elevated relative to the peer median. Key uncertainties include the sustainability of frontier-tech valuations amid market volatility and the representativeness of the peer set, which spans a 26x spread. Further refinement would require clarity on unit economics and revenue growth relative to peers.
